data.title: As a plugin maintainer, I can see review-caught bug counts and tier/overlay analytics
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend review-stats.sh with the compound heuristic for review-caught-bugs-not-caught-by-tests and tier/overlay analytics
**Why**: The compound heuristic is the highest-value metric — it quantifies the unique value of LLM reviews beyond what automated testing catches
**Scope**:
- IN: Compound heuristic (green test gate + correctness/verification dimension + critical/major/important/severe severity + code-change resolution), tier usage distribution, escalation frequency, security overlay trigger count, performance overlay trigger count
- OUT: Basic reporting (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, review-stats.sh reports review-caught-bugs-not-caught-by-tests count using the compound heuristic consuming test_gate_status from review events
  <- Satisfies: 'The review-caught bug metric uses a compound heuristic'
- When this story is complete, review-stats.sh reports tier usage distribution, escalation frequency, and security/performance overlay trigger counts
  <- Satisfies: 'tier usage and escalation rates, overlay trigger frequency'
- When this story is complete, unit tests verify the heuristic with fixtures: (a) meets all 4 criteria -> counted, (b) failing tests -> not counted, (c) defense resolution -> not counted, (d) hygiene dimension -> not counted
  <- Satisfies: unit test DoD

## Considerations

- [Testing] Compound heuristic needs realistic multi-source fixtures (test gate status + finding data + resolution action)
- [Data] test_gate_status field must be present in review events (emitted by Story 2)
